learning_objectives: Describe the Nurse Assistant's role in collecting specimens (sputum, urine, and/or stool)
Discuss procedures for bed making and maintaining proper body mechanics
Discuss types of beds and bed positions
Discuss ways to maintain the patient's/resident's environment
Describe the Nurse Assistant's role in administering an enema
Describe the Nurse Assistant's role in giving a suppository
List the types and uses of gastrointestinal (GI) tubes
Describe major nursing care activities for patients/residents with feeding tube (nasogastric or gastrostomy)
Describe nursing care activities for a patient/resident receiving intravenous (I.V.) therapy
Describe the Nursing Assistant's role in assisting the patient/resident to maintain fluid balance
Describe the purpose and procedure for measuring the amount of fluid taken in and fluids excreted by the patient/resident
Discuss the Nursing's Assistant role in the use of bandages, binders and dressings
Describe the use and method of applying anti-embolic hose/elastic stockings
Identify the Nurse Assistant's role in the care of patients/residents' skin conditions and the use of non-prescription ointments, lotions, or powders
Explain the role of the Nurse Assistant in the admission of a patient/resident to the facility
Explain the role of the Nurse Assistant in transferring a patient/resident from one area to another within the facility
Explain the role of the Nurse Assistant in the discharge of a patient/resident
